_meta.label: Skeleton Swap
_meta.summary: Terra DEX hosting some TLA pools (typically XYK). We price its pools via the warlock backend using the reserve-ratio anchor method. Same pair can exist on both Astroport and SkeletonSwap, splitting liquidity.

facts.claim: SkeletonSwap pool data comes from the warlock backend at dex.warlock.backbonelabs.io/api/pools/phoenix-1, returning pools with token_0/token_1, reserves, decimals, and tvl_usd.

We price SkeletonSwap tokens by the anchor method: price_token = (reserve_anchor / reserve_token) × anchor_price, using LUNA/USDC/USDt as trusted anchors. This is valid because SS pools are constant-product (XYK).
facts.notes: XYK mechanism is the same across DEXes; we cite Astroport's clear writeup for the reserve-ratio=price rule. The naive tvl/2 method is WRONG (gives one token two prices) — anchor method validated 7/8 within 3%.

facts.claim: Near-empty pools produce noise prices (a pool with ~$0.16 of liquidity manufactured a $43k wBTC price). We skip pools below a $500 TVL floor so dust pools cannot produce fake prices.
facts.notes: Our own pricing rule, derived from observed dust-pool failures. Floor reduced noisy SS prices from 24 to 10.

facts.claim: The same token pair can have pools on BOTH Astroport and SkeletonSwap, splitting liquidity between venues. This is why our catalog tracks pools per-DEX and why a token may have multiple pool-implied prices.
facts.notes: Stated in our curated protocols.json; canonical SS doc link to be confirmed.
